title: Echinopsis multiangularis
intro: Echinopsis multiangularis, also known as the San Pedro cactus, is a species of cactus in the genus Echinopsis, native to the Andean region of South America. This plant is believed to have various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing stress and improving cognitive function. The bioactive constituents of Echinopsis multiangularis include alkaloids, glycosides, and flavonoids, including mescaline, a psychoactive compound responsible for its medicinal and spiritual properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Echinopsis multiangularis include dried cactus extracts, tea infusions, and capsules, which are used in traditional medicine for their potential therapeutic benefits.
taxonomy: Echinopsis multiangularis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the cactus family, Cactaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Cactoideae and the tribe Cacteae. Within the Cacteae tribe, Echinopsis multiangularis is a member of the genus Echinopsis. The genus Echinopsis is a group of cacti that are primarily found in South America, with some species being native to other parts of the world. This classification helps to group similar species and understand their evolutionary relationships within the plant kingdom.
